lessons.problemStatement:
Predict the output for this What is Java? program.

Write a Java program that prints exactly: Welcome to Java!
constraints: Use only What is Java? concepts covered in Week 1.
Program must compile and run with the given values.
lessons.stepByStepExplanation: This exercise reinforces What is Java? fundamentals.

approaches.name: Brute Force
approaches.description: Linear scan or nested loops — correct but slow.

approaches.name: Optimal
approaches.description: Direct pattern application with minimal extra work.

lessons.dryRun: Trace the program and confirm output: Welcome to Java!
alternativeSolutions: Use formatted printf instead of println
followUpQuestions: How would you extend this with Scanner input?

lessons.title: Right Triangle Star Pattern
lessons.description: Print a right triangle of stars with rows growing from 1 to n.
lessons.explanation: Use two loops: one for rows and one for columns. In each cell, decide whether to print a token based on row/column rules for right-star-triangle.
commonMistakes: Printing trailing spaces and failing output match.
Mixing row and column indices while checking conditions.
interviewTips: Explain the matrix view even for triangle patterns.
Name conditions clearly: border, diagonal, center.
practiceQuestions: Mirror the pattern horizontally.
Replace stars with row index.
hints: Write the row condition first, then token value.
Test with n=1 and n=2 before larger values.

executionTrace.action: Initialize n and enter outer loop
Build current row string using inner loop

lessons.title: Majority Element
lessons.description:
Find the element that appears more than n/2 times (guaranteed to exist).

lessons.description: Apply this in a food delivery platform backend. Pick top earners per department using window function.

lessons.description: Apply this in a expense tracker app backend. Count employees per department.

SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) AS headcount FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.dept_id GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY d.name;
